text: Amarienborg
Amarienborg is the home of the Danish royal family. It is an impressive sight to behold with its beautiful architecture. At 12:00, there will be the change of guard which is a must-see.
$1.25 (reservation required)
All week
Frederiks Kirke
Known for its stunning modern architecture, Frederiks Kirke is a popular spot for tourists.
Tuesday to Friday
The Little Mermaid Statue
This statue is based on the character from Grimm's Fairy Tales and is one of the most iconic sights in Copenhagen.
Free
All week
Noma
Rundetaarn
This 17th-century astronomy tower offers a unique insight into the scientific history of Denmark.
DKK 40
All week
Christiansborg Palace
One of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.
Free
Tuesday to Sunday
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K, so you might need to bring an adapter.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros, so it's a good idea to have some local currency on hand.
Copenhagen has a total of 6 metro lines, making it easy to get around the city.
The weather in Copenhagen in December is below -15 degrees Celsius. Make sure to pack warm clothes!
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen.
Remember, the “hello” in Danish is “hej”, “thank you” is “farvel”, and “goodbye” is “tak”.
National Museum of Denmark
As the country’s largest cultural history museum, it offers an extensive collection of artifacts and exhibits that showcase Denmark’s rich history.
Tuesday to Sunday

In Denmark, tipping is not obligatory but is appreciated for good service. However, keep in mind that the tip rate in Copenhagen is over 50%.

text: Danish people appreciate personal space. It's common to stand a bit farther apart from someone when speaking to them than in other cultures.
